title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
authors: Jared Kaplan, Sam McCandlish, Tom Henighan, Tom B. Brown et al.
hook: Model performance becomes surprisingly predictable as you scale up size, data, and compute.
promise: You will understand how model performance can be forecasted using simple scaling laws.
prerequisite_note: If you know that neural networks get better with more data or compute, you are ready.

glossary.plain: A mathematical rule showing how performance changes as you increase model size, data, or compute.
A measure of how well a model predicts the correct output, lower means better predictions.
A relationship where one quantity changes as a fixed power of another.
When a model learns the training data too well and performs poorly on new data.
How effectively a model learns from each data point.
The total amount of computational resources available for training a model.

cards.title: How big is enough?
body: Learn how simple rules predict language model performance as you scale up.

cards.title: Why did scaling become a science?
body: For years, training bigger neural networks felt like guesswork. Sometimes adding data or compute helped, sometimes it barely mattered.
This paper revealed that performance follows a predictable curve as you scale up model size, data, and compute, making progress feel almost mathematical.

cards.title: How scaling predicts performance
body: Start with a small language model and a modest dataset. Measure its performance using cross-entropy loss.
Increase the model size, the dataset, or the compute, and watch the loss drop in a smooth, predictable way following a power law.
This lets you forecast how much bigger or better you need to go for the next performance jump.
cards.middle: Scale up size, data, and compute
cards.caption: Scaling up resources leads to steady, forecastable gains in model performance.

cards.title: Guessing vs. forecasting model success
body: Before scaling laws, researchers had to experiment blindly to find the best model size.
cards.onNote: Simple math predicts how much to scale for better performance

cards.title: The central trick: power laws everywhere
body: The authors found that as you increase model size, dataset size, or compute, the loss decreases smoothly, following a power law.
This means you can use a simple formula to estimate how much improvement to expect from more resources, making scaling a science instead of a gamble.

cards.title: How does model size affect data needs?
body: Slide to see how changing model size changes how much data you need before overfitting.
cards.low: Needs less data, but quickly hits a performance wall.
cards.mid: Balanced size, uses data efficiently before overfitting.
cards.high: Needs much more data, but gains more from each sample before overfitting.

cards.title: Pick your scaling recipe
body: Choose which ingredients matter most to improve a language model's performance.

choices.label: Model size
choices.note: Bigger models can learn more patterns.

choices.label: Compute budget
choices.note: More compute allows longer or bigger training.

choices.label: Training duration
choices.note: Stopping at the right time avoids overfitting.

choices.label: Network width
choices.note: Wider networks matter less than total size.

choices.label: Network depth
choices.note: Deeper networks matter less than total size.

cards.title: Scaling laws change the game
body: Before this paper, model improvement felt like trial and error, with no clear path forward.
Now, teams can plan model growth and resource allocation with confidence, accelerating advances in language models and AI.

bars.label: Sample efficiency
bars.note: Larger models learn more per sample

cards.caption: Scaling laws made performance gains more predictable and efficient than ever before.

cards.title: Why scaling laws are essential
body: Scaling laws transformed AI development from intuition and luck to a more predictable engineering discipline.
By showing that bigger models and datasets yield steady, forecastable improvements, this paper laid the groundwork for today's AI breakthroughs.

recap.label: Observe
recap.note: Notice smooth performance gains as you scale up.

recap.label: Measure
recap.note: Use cross-entropy loss to track improvements.

recap.label: Forecast
recap.note: Apply power laws to predict future gains.

recap.label: Allocate
recap.note: Plan resources for optimal model training.

cards.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
body: Read the original paper to see the equations and evidence behind these scaling laws.
